data.body: The claim command works today only because workflow profiles alternate queue (ready_for_X) and action (X) states, so next_happy_path_state from an action state never lands on another action state. But this is accidental — the guard should explicitly check that the knot is in a claimable queue state (ready_for_*) before advancing, rather than relying on the shape of the workflow graph. If a profile ever defined adjacent action states, claim would incorrectly advance through them.
data.title: claim should guard on queue state, not rely on happy-path traversal
